automechs.configuration.title: Automechs Config
gui.automechs.mech_database.empty: No mechs found. Build one at the Robot Builder.
gui.automechs.mech_database.name: Name
gui.automechs.mech_database.select_hint: Drag to box-select · Shift-drag a robot out of its group
gui.automechs.mech_database.title: Mech Dashboard
gui.automechs.schematic.empty: No schematics found. Click "Open Folder" and drop .litematic / .nbt files in.
gui.automechs.schematic.title: Select Schematic
gui.automechs.terminal.empty: Network is empty — insert items below.
gui.automechs.terminal.title: Storage Terminal
guide.automechs.builder.body: Load a .litematic or .nbt schematic, place its ghost (scroll = distance, R = rotate, X/Z = up/down), and it builds block by block, pulling materials from any chest in the footprint. Materials lists what it needs. It clears terrain but spares chests.
guide.automechs.builder.title: Builder Mech
guide.automechs.builder_bench.body: Place this where it has a clear 3×3×3 space — it forms a fabrication frame. Slot the three parts plus a role Circuit and power it: a mech assembles and deploys, bound to you. Sneak + right-click a mech to pack it back into a chassis item (upgrades kept).
guide.automechs.builder_bench.title: Robot Builder
guide.automechs.cache_crawler.body: A little spider-bot that keeps your storage tidy. It scuttles across nearby Data Racks and defragments them — merging scattered fragments of the same item into full stacks and compacting them to the front, freeing sectors. Craft a few from an AI Chip, place them near your racks, and they maintain the network on their own.
guide.automechs.cache_crawler.title: Cache Crawler
guide.automechs.charging_pad.body: Feed it FE from any cable and it pushes charge into every mech standing nearby. Mechs route their recharge step here automatically. A Capacitor module makes its battery bigger; a Range Extender widens its charge radius.
guide.automechs.charging_pad.title: Charging Pad
guide.automechs.circuits.body: A circuit sets a mech's job. Fabricate the one you want — Mining, Farming, Building or Combat — and slot it into the Robot Builder's circuit socket when assembling. Hover each to read its role. The following pages cover every role in turn.
guide.automechs.circuits.title: Role Circuits
guide.automechs.combat.body: A combat mech guards a post: it fights mobs in its radius then returns. Set the radius and Foes/All in its screen. With a Soul Tank it banks XP from kills — click the green XP tank to drain it into yourself.
guide.automechs.combat.title: Combat Mech
guide.automechs.conduit.body: Cheap cable that carries FE between generators, Charging Pads and machines. One craft makes six. Lay a line from your Dynamo to a Charging Pad so your mechs always have a place to top up. Out of power a mech simply pauses — it never dies or griefs.
guide.automechs.conduit.title: Power Conduit
guide.automechs.contents.title: Contents
guide.automechs.data_cable.body: Cheap network wiring — one craft makes eight. Cables connect the Main Drive to its Data Racks and the Storage Terminal; everything joined by an unbroken cable run is one network. Lay them through your storage room to wire it all together.
guide.automechs.data_cable.title: Data Cable
guide.automechs.data_rack.body: The storage cabinet. Each rack holds many 'data sectors'; inserted items scatter across free sectors, so a rack fragments as it fills — its face shows Defrag% and item count. Add more racks for more capacity. Fragmented racks waste space until a Cache Crawler compacts them.
guide.automechs.data_rack.title: Data Rack
guide.automechs.fabricator.body: Your starting machine. Feed it raw materials plus FE and it fabricates the mech parts and role circuits. Open it, drop ingredients in, and let power do the work. Build this first — everything else is made here or from its parts.
guide.automechs.fabricator.title: Fabricator
guide.automechs.farming.body: Press Set Area and aim at your field. It harvests ripe crops, replants seeds it carries, and (with a Fertilizer module) bone-meals young crops, depositing produce to a chest. The green outline marks the field.
guide.automechs.farming.title: Farming Mech
guide.automechs.holo.body: This manual. Cheap to craft — a book, redstone and glowstone — so you can read it from the very start. Right-click to open it anywhere. Keep it handy while you learn the ropes; every machine, role and upgrade is indexed on the Contents page.
guide.automechs.holo.title: Holo-Guide
guide.automechs.linker.body: The targeting wand. Right-click a chest to set a mech's deposit/withdraw chest, and use it to place a Mining quarry box or a Farming field. Sneak + right-click resets a link. Keep one in your hotbar — it is how you point mechs at the world.
guide.automechs.linker.title: Mech Linker
guide.automechs.main_drive.body: The network's controller and power supply. Feed it Forge Energy from any cable and it floods the connected Data Cables to find and run your Racks and Terminal. Its draw scales with how many racks and how much data it powers; if its buffer runs dry the network goes offline (stored items stay safe). Its face shows live status.
guide.automechs.main_drive.title: Main Drive
guide.automechs.mining.body: Open it and press Quarry, then aim to place a box: it digs the area top-down, stores drops, deposits to a linked chest, and recharges at a Charging Pad. The cyan outline shows the dig volume (toggle with the eye). Range upgrades enlarge it.
guide.automechs.mining.title: Mining Mech
guide.automechs.overview.body: Automechs are mobile worker robots you build, power with Forge Energy, and send out to mine, farm, build and guard. Fabricate and assemble them, slot a role circuit to pick the job, then upgrade and command your fleet. Click a Contents entry to jump, or use the arrows / scroll wheel to page.
guide.automechs.overview.title: Welcome to Automechs
guide.automechs.part_chip.body: The mech's brain — it's also what a Mech Tablet needs. Fabricate it from gold, lapis and redstone. With plates, a core and a chip in hand, head to the Robot Builder.
guide.automechs.part_chip.title: AI Chip
guide.automechs.part_core.body: The mech's energy heart: it holds the FE battery that everything runs on. Fabricate it from redstone, copper and a diamond. One Power Core per mech.
guide.automechs.part_core.title: Power Core
guide.automechs.part_plates.body: The mech's hull. Fabricate a set from iron and copper in the Fabricator (raw materials + FE) — you need one set for every mech. This is the body the Robot Builder wraps around the core and chip.
guide.automechs.part_plates.title: Chassis Plates
guide.automechs.power.body: Everything runs on Forge Energy (FE). The Combustion Dynamo burns ordinary furnace fuel (coal, charcoal, planks, lava bucket) to generate FE, and pauses itself when its buffer is full. Any FE generator from any mod works too — there are no hard deps.
guide.automechs.power.title: Combustion Dynamo
guide.automechs.storage.body: A second kind of automation: an AE2-style storage network running on Forge Energy. A Main Drive powers a web of Data Cables that link Data Racks (the storage) to a Storage Terminal (your access screen). Racks fragment as they fill, and little Cache Crawler bots scuttle over and defragment them. No power, no access — but your items always stay safe.
guide.automechs.storage.title: Data Storage Network
guide.automechs.storage_terminal.body: Your window into the whole network: an ME-style grid showing every item aggregated across all racks. Shift-click from your inventory to store; click an item to pull a stack out. The left toolbar sorts by amount, name or mod. Needs the Main Drive online to work.
guide.automechs.storage_terminal.title: Storage Terminal
guide.automechs.tablet.body: Your fleet command center: shift + right-click a robot to register it, then right-click the air for a dashboard of every mech — rename, group, pause, recall or inspect at a glance. Needs an AI Chip to craft.
guide.automechs.tablet.title: Mech Tablet
guide.automechs.upgrade_station.body: Pack a mech into its chassis and place it on the right, a module on the left, then power the station: the module is stamped onto the chassis. Redeploy to apply. The next pages list every module — all share one recipe: a Copper + Redstone frame around a catalyst.
guide.automechs.upgrade_station.title: Upgrade Station
guide.automechs.upgrades_combat.title: Combat Modules
guide.automechs.upgrades_core.title: Core Modules
guide.automechs.upgrades_field.title: Field & Utility Modules
guide.automechs.upgrades_mining.title: Mining Modules
message.automechs.build_aim: Placing %s · scroll: distance · R: rotate · X/Z: up/down · left-click: build · right-click: cancel
message.automechs.build_set: Build placed — the mech is on it.
message.automechs.builder_no_room: Not enough room — the Robot Builder needs a clear 3×3×3 space.
message.automechs.farm_aim: Aim and left-click to set the farm field · right-click to cancel
message.automechs.farm_set: Farm field set — the mech is on it.
message.automechs.quarry_aim: Aim and left-click to start the quarry · right-click to cancel
message.automechs.quarry_set: Quarry set — the mech is on it.
message.automechs.schematic_empty: That schematic has no blocks to build.
message.automechs.tablet_already: That robot is already on the tablet
tooltip.automechs.chassis_deploy: Use on a block to deploy a mech
tooltip.automechs.circuit_use: Slot into the Robot Builder's Circuit socket
tooltip.automechs.holo_guide: Right-click to open the field manual
tooltip.automechs.scope_pad: Also works in the Charging Pad
tooltip.automechs.tablet_add: Shift + right-click a robot to add it
tooltip.automechs.tablet_use: Right-click to open the node dashboard
upgrade.automechs.armor.desc: Adds hull plating for more maximum health.
upgrade.automechs.capacity.desc: Bigger energy battery: the mech (or Charging Pad) stores more FE.
upgrade.automechs.efficiency.desc: Lowers the FE cost of every action (per block mined, crop tended, hit landed).
upgrade.automechs.ender_link.desc: Deposits into the linked chest from any distance: no walking back.
upgrade.automechs.fertilizer.desc: Lets a Farming mech use bone meal from its inventory to speed up crops.
upgrade.automechs.fire_aspect.desc: Attacks set targets on fire, so drops come pre-cooked.
upgrade.automechs.fortune.desc: Ore blocks drop extra raw ore and gems, like a Fortune pickaxe.
upgrade.automechs.hazard_seal.desc: Works safely around water and lava and lights its dig site, so a quarry won't flood, burn or spawn mobs.
upgrade.automechs.looting.desc: Mobs drop more loot, like a Looting sword.
upgrade.automechs.magnet.desc: Vacuums up nearby dropped items into the mech's inventory.
upgrade.automechs.range.desc: Enlarges the work area and reach: bigger quarry and field; in a Charging Pad, a wider charge radius.
upgrade.automechs.silk_touch.desc: Mines blocks whole: ore blocks, glass, ice. Overrides Fortune.
upgrade.automechs.smelter.desc: Auto-smelts mined ores into ingots. Stacks with Fortune.
upgrade.automechs.solar.desc: Slowly recharges the mech's battery in daylight.
upgrade.automechs.soul_tank.desc: Greatly increases how much kill-XP a combat mech can hold. Right-click it empty-handed to drain the XP into you.
upgrade.automechs.speed.desc: Acts and moves faster: quicker digging, harvesting and attacks, plus faster walking.
